title: #product: 1743300123
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads-up 1: we need to finalize the mini-wave for post-launch next sprint. Customer impact signals suggest prioritizing reliability and a few rapid UX wins. Proposal: 70% bugs, 30% enhancements. Anyone opposed?

Ben Cho: 70/30 seems reasonable given the telemetry spikes. Curious what the bug shortlist looks like though.

Nina Patel: Agree on the split. Also request we include one experiment in enhancements that we can measure in 2 weeks (not more). Sam can own the metric.

Sam Ortiz (PMM): I can own the experiment metric. Suggest we A/B test the onboarding CTA (two copy variants) and measure 7-day activation. Low risk and fast.

Priya K (Eng): Capacity check: 4 devs available Tue-Thu. If we do 70/30, I'd allocate 3 on bugs and 1 on enhancements/experiment. Need QA coverage too.

Ava Chen: Good. Constraints:
- Freeze database schema migrations for this wave.
- No new heavy infra work (keep deploy surface small).
- All tickets must include an "impact_statement" and a short rollback plan.

Ben Cho: Draft bug list (short):
```
- BUG-5120: prompt truncation for >64k inputs (P0) 1 customer-reported
- BUG-5175: /embeddings latency regressions under 95th pct (P0)
- BUG-5201: auth token rotation race condition (P1)
- BUG-5233: dashboard refresh shows stale metrics for 30s (P1)
- BUG-5300: autoscaler mislabels idle pods leading to scale-down (P2)
```
I'll add impact_statement + acceptance tests by EOD.

Nina Patel: For the UX experiment: ENH-3301 onboarding CTA copy variants + tracking. Sam will define the KPI and sample size. Keep the experiment to 5% traffic.

Sam Ortiz (PMM): I'll push the KPI: 7d activation increase, 95% CI, min N=3k. If we can't meet sample in 2 weeks, we roll experiment back.

Priya K (Eng): Patch branch: patch/miniwave-3301. I'll create PRs for each P0 first. CI will run nightly. QA-bot: can you run targeted regressions for embeddings and auth?

QA-bot: regression-suite queued for patch/miniwave-3301 -> https://ci.redwood.ai/runs/miniwave-3301 (logs attached). Initial failures: BUG-5175 latency spike reprobed, attaching perf trace.

Ben Cho: Acked. Priya, can you take BUG-5175 first, then BUG-5120?

Priya K (Eng): Yes, starting on 5175. ETA 1.5 days for root cause + patch. If root cause is infra-related, may need ops assist.

Ava Chen: Decision checklist before we ship canary:
- Finalized ticket list & owners (Ben)
- impact_statement + acceptance tests in each ticket (owners)
- patch branch + preview build (Priya)
- experiment gating (Sam) + analytics dashboard (Nina)
- rollback window scheduled (Thu 10:00-12:00 UTC)

Ben Cho: I'll post updated ticket list and owners by 3pm.

Sam Ortiz (PMM): I'll set up the dashboard and the experiment flag in prod with 5% gating.

Nina Patel: Quick ask 1 can we keep a hotfix pager rotation for the first 48h after canary? Last time we needed fast patching.

Ava Chen: Yes, create a 48h hotfix rota. Ben, please coordinate with on-call.

Priya K (Eng): Created patch + PRs. Link: https://git.redwood.ai/r/redwood/miniwave-3301. Reviewer assigned: Dave (if available).

Ben Cho: No objections. Locking the 70/30 split and the plan above. Will follow up with stakeholder summary. :white_check_mark:

Sam Ortiz (PMM): Thanks team. I'll monitor the experiment funnel and report daily.

Ava Chen: Great. If anything urgent pops up, ping here or DM.

title: #announcements: 1741239999
preview:
Ava Chen: Morning — quick exec micro-brief. Thanks for the steady ops last week. Two asks: 1) tighten cost targets for hosted tier by 8% y/y on per-token spend — ops+eng, prioritize caching and batching recommendations; 2) we need SLA recalibration for Dedicated customers in EU region after infra change. Details below.

Diego Morales: Ack. Eng will push a batching/caching quick playbook today. Can we relax p99 from 220ms -> 250ms temporarily while we apply kernel patches? Need signoff from product/ops.

Riley Gupta: Product supports a short p99 relaxation for the next 2 weeks if we: a) notify impacted customers, b) enable automatic fallbacks to lower-cost model variants. I'll own customer comms with CS.

Samir Patel: For kernel patches, can we schedule a rolling canary tonight 02:00-04:00 PST? deploy-bot will manage. Patch reduces memory pressure but needs a DB migration in read-only mode.

deploy-bot: Scheduled canary deploy: canary-2024-1741239999 -> infra: gpu-pool-3 | window 02:00-04:00 PST | rollback on error. Link: https://deploys.redwood/example/1741239999

Maya Li: Legal/Compliance: note on data-retention policy — any change to caching must still honor per-customer retention TTLs and KMS scoping. Ops, please confirm no cached data is stored outside requested TTLs.

Diego Morales: Confirmed — caching will be prefix-only and honor TTL. We'll add telemetry to show cache-hit demographics. Patch includes flag: cache.store_policy=prefix_ttl.

Kara O'Neil: Heads up — we're deferring the company-wide all-hands to Monday to give teams time to adjust. People Ops will send updated calendar.

Ava Chen: Thanks all. Final asks: Diego/Riley — get a customer-facing note by EOD PST; Samir — proceed with canary but pause migration if error >= 3% of requests; Maya — confirm compliance signoff by EOD. I'll review metrics Tues morning. :thumbsup: 

Riley Gupta: On it, drafting message now.

Maya Li: Compliance signoff issued, pending small audit — will post evidence in #compliance by EOD.

Samir Patel: Canary started. Monitoring dashboard: https://metrics.redwood/canary/1741239999 — will post updates here.

title: #eng-releases: 1740717722
preview:
Connor O'Brien: need final eyes on runtime release notes blurb for cache telemetry rollout. goal: accurate + not scary. also confirm feature flag defaults for 1.18.0.

Connor O'Brien: current draft in PR-18435 has:
- “Adds prefix/KV cache hit-rate, eviction pressure, and wasted prefill metrics (opt-in via flags).”
- “New miss reason breakdown (capacity/ttl_expired/policy_disabled/hash_mismatch/version_mismatch).”
- “Sampling + rate limiting for high-volume cache events.”

Connor O'Brien: questions:
1) default flags in prod? staging? dedicated?
2) do we call out dashboards exist (internal) vs Console (not yet)?
3) do we mention metric name change / deprecations now or later?

Omar El-Sayed: for defaults: i strongly prefer OFF by default in prod for first cut, ON in staging + canary. instrumentation is low overhead but we’re still validating ingestion/cardinality in the wild.

Omar El-Sayed: flags from PR-18429:
- `runtime.cache_telemetry.enabled` (master)
- `runtime.cache_telemetry.sample_rate` (float 0..1)
- `runtime.cache_telemetry.emit_miss_reasons` (extra labels)
- `runtime.cache_telemetry.emit_histograms` (prefix reuse depth + token buckets)

Omar El-Sayed: we can set enabled=true in canary only, sample_rate=0.01 in prod once we turn it on. miss reasons + histograms should be behind their own flags too.

Paula Mendes: from product side: please do NOT say “Console support” yet. we’re only doing internal dashboards + maybe an internal report. wording should set expectation that this is internal observability + rollout guardrails.

Paula Mendes: recommend: “New internal cache efficiency telemetry (behind flags) to support ongoing cost/latency optimization work.”

Marcus Reed: +1 to conservative defaults. perf canary results were clean at 1% sample rate, but we didn’t run at 100% for long. i’d do:
- staging: enabled=true, sample_rate=1.0
- perf-canary/prod-canary: enabled=true, sample_rate=0.25 (or 0.1)
- prod: enabled=false (initial), then enabled=true sample_rate=0.01 after 24h clean canary

Marcus Reed: also pls include a line “no raw prompts logged; metrics are bucketed” (we keep getting questions from sec review).

Allison Grant: for deprecations: we did the rename in PR-18431. dashboards are already updated on observability-pack side, but release notes should mention “old metric names deprecated; see internal metrics guide” so oncall doesn’t get confused if they have local grafana panels.

Allison Grant: maybe a short line: “cache metrics aligned to naming/cardinality policy; previous experimental metric names will stop emitting in a future release.” avoid promising exact removal date.

Nadia Rahman: on “emit_miss_reasons”: be careful. that label set is acceptable only with the route/model allowlist and prompt length bucketing. we should not encourage folks to enable miss reasons in prod without also having ingestion guards deployed.

Nadia Rahman: release notes should point to the rollout runbook / checklist (internal link) + call out that enabling miss reasons increases series count.

Connor O'Brien: ok so proposal:
- defaults in 1.18.0: prod `runtime.cache_telemetry.enabled=false`
- staging: enabled=true sample_rate=1
- canary: enabled=true sample_rate=0.1
- `emit_miss_reasons=false` by default everywhere, `emit_histograms=true` only in staging/canary?

Omar El-Sayed: i’d rather histograms default off too in prod/canary until we confirm bucket sanity. staging yes.

Marcus Reed: agree. histograms are the easiest way to accidentally crank ingestion volume.

Paula Mendes: wording-wise: make it sound like a gradual rollout. also clarify “cache hit-rate” here includes prefix reuse + kv reuse (per ADR) so ppl don’t compare to old definition.

Allison Grant: pls add: “Known limitation: metrics may appear lower during streaming retries; fixed in PR-18428” (or just “retry handling improved”). depends on whether 18428 is in this release.

Connor O'Brien: 18428 is in, yes.

Ava Chen: jumping in — keep the release note minimal + link out. we don’t want customers reading internal release notes and thinking they need to do something. tone: “adds instrumentation; off by default; can be enabled for debugging/analysis.”

Connor O'Brien: got it. updated release note snippet draft:
“Cache telemetry (experimental): Adds new prefix/KV cache efficiency metrics (hit-rate, miss reasons, evictions/pressure, wasted prefill) to support internal observability and optimization work. Off by default in production; enabled in staging/canary per rollout plan. Telemetry is privacy-safe (no raw prompts) and uses bucketing/sampling to control overhead. Note: enabling miss-reason labels and histograms increases metric cardinality; follow the cache telemetry rollout runbook.”

Connor O'Brien: plus short bullet: “Metric names aligned to cache telemetry naming standards; previous experimental names are deprecated and will be removed in a future release.”

Nadia Rahman: that’s good. small tweak: say “miss reason breakdown (optional)” instead of implying it always emits.

Omar El-Sayed: +1. also make sure we list the actual config keys somewhere (maybe “see runtime config docs”). folks will ask in incident.

Allison Grant: add a one-liner “See: dashboards → Cache Efficiency / Prefix+KV” (internal) if release notes are internal-facing only.

Paula Mendes: ok if internal release notes. please don’t mention Console.

Connor O'Brien: confirmed these are internal runtime release notes.

DeployBot: buildkite #runtime-main — Release Notes Check: PR-18435 updated by @connor. awaiting approvals.

Connor O'Brien: final: setting defaults:
- prod: `runtime.cache_telemetry.enabled=false`
- prod (when enabled): `sample_rate=0.01`, `emit_miss_reasons=false`, `emit_histograms=false`
- canary: `enabled=true`, `sample_rate=0.1`, others false
- staging: `enabled=true`, `sample_rate=1.0`, `emit_histograms=true`, `emit_miss_reasons=true` (ok?)

Nadia Rahman: staging miss reasons ok. just make sure observability-pack PR-613 (cardinality guards) is already deployed to staging ingestion.

Allison Grant: it is in staging as of yesterday. prod deploy scheduled after canary.

Marcus Reed: defaults look sane. perf canary gate will catch if overhead surprises.

Omar El-Sayed: 👍 works for runtime.

Paula Mendes: 👍

Ava Chen: approve. make sure the runbook link is the current one.

Connor O'Brien: cool, pushing update + tagging for approvals.

title: #announcements: 1739308055
preview:
Talia Benson: :mega: Internal preview: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO

We’re ready for an internal preview of the Dedicated tiering + throughput SLO work.

What this is:
• A customer-facing tier taxonomy (Standard / Plus / Premium) with explicit entitlements
• A formal throughput SLO definition + measurement semantics for Dedicated
• Initial shape catalog + tier→shape mapping guidance (by region)
• Draft enablement assets (cheat sheet, PRD/FAQ) + escalation + comms plan for SLO misses

What this is NOT (yet):
• Final contract language / order form text (Legal review in progress)
• Public docs GA (docs draft exists but not ready to share externally)
• “Promise any number you want” — please do not invent custom commitments. Use the tier guidance.

Where to read (internal only):
• PRD (Dedicated throughput SLO + tiers): sources/confluence/product-docs/requirements/dedicated-throughput-slo-prd.json
• Formal SLO definition (metrics/exclusions/alerts): sources/confluence/eng-sre/slo-and-error-budgets/dedicated-throughput-slo-definition.json
• Tier pricing proposal (v1): sources/confluence/product-docs/pricing-and-packaging/dedicated-pricing-tiers-v1.json
• Seller cheat sheet draft: sources/confluence/sales-enablement/pricing-guidance/dedicated-tier-cheat-sheet.json
• Shape catalog (Dedicated pools by region): sources/confluence/eng-infra/gpu-fleet-and-capacity/hardware-shape-catalog-dedicated.json

High-level decisions (preview):
• Canonical throughput metric: tokens/sec attributed per Dedicated deployment (streaming-safe accounting)
• Measurement window: rolling windows with burn-rate alerting (details in SLO doc)
• Exclusions: customer-side throttling/backpressure, unsupported model/version/ctx settings, and warmup periods (see SLO doc for exact wording)
• Tiers:
  - Standard: throughput floor on a single region pool, best-effort burst, standard support
  - Plus: higher floor + defined burst policy, faster response targets, stronger isolation assumptions
  - Premium: highest floor, warm pool/spare capacity posture, most conservative overload behavior, fastest response targets

Timeline (current plan):
• This week: internal preview + feedback window
• Next 1–2 weeks: finalize tier entitlements/exclusions + pricing guardrails + shape list per region
• Enablement: Sales/CS training sessions start next week (calendar invite from @Ava Chen)
• Limited GA: tiered SLO rollout to a small set of Dedicated customers (beta list being finalized)

Where to send questions / feedback:
• Product/tier definitions: @Lauren Bishop (reply in this thread is fine)
• SLO semantics / measurement / alerts: @Sean Gallagher + @Rafael Mendes
• Shapes / regions / lead times: @Irene Choi + @Vanessa Ortiz
• Pricing/discount guardrails: @Isabelle Laurent (Sales Ops) + @Laura Bennett (Finance, async)
• Enablement materials: @Ava Chen + @Elliot Price

Please keep customer-facing statements to the approved tier language only. If a customer asks for a “throughput guarantee in writing,” route to Lauren + Commercial counsel; we’ll provide the reviewed language.

:thread: Reply here with questions/edge cases you want covered in training.

—

Stephanie Nguyen: Thanks Talia. For AEs: are we ok telling customers “tokens/sec is the commitment” vs “requests/sec”? We’ve got folks quoting QPS today.

title: #eng-runtime: 1732109876
preview:
Ava Chen: Nightly perf job artifacts size spiked — nsys captures for the KV-heavy smoke runs are ~1.6GB each and uploads are timing out. Seen this on the new kvcache evictions tests. Anyone else?
Samir Patel: Yup. We capture a lot (full cuda context + nvtx + memory). We should trim capture scope and only collect full nsys when the run looks anomalous. :eyes:
Liam O'Neill: What exactly is CI running atm? can you paste the command?
Ava Chen: Current CI snippet (from circle job):
```bash
nsys profile --output ${ARTIFACT} --trace=cuda,nvtx --force-overwrite 
  --stats=true 
  --stop-on-exit 
  python run_kvcache_smoke.py --iters 100
```
Samir Patel: Ok. A few levers: 1) restrict trace range (e.g. --capture-range=nvtx) 2) disable heavy memory tracking 3) use nsys --compress or switch to perfetto for lightweight timeline. Also consider sampling only when kv miss rate > threshold.
Julie Ramos: We tried compressing but the diff dashboard can't meaningfully diff compressed blobs. We need a strategy: keep small daily artifacts for diffs and keep one raw artifact per N nights for deep inspection.
perf-ci-bot: perf-ci-bot: Proposal: store full nsys trace for 1/7 nightly runs (weekly baseline) and lightweight perfetto+kernel-timeline JSON for daily runs. Upload prefix: s3://redwood-perf/nightly/1732109876/ (example).
Samir Patel: Also noticed kernel-selection noise — tiny ordering differences in kernels generate huge binary diffs. Suggest we canonicalize kernel timeline before computing diffs: group by NVTX op id and sort kernels by start time within op, then normalize timestamps to op-relative.
Ava Chen: Can we make the sampler only capture full nsys when KV cache miss ratio > 5%? That would focus storage on problematic runs.
Liam O'Neill: We already emit metrics.csv from runs with fields: total_requests, cache_hits, cache_misses. We can compute kv_miss_ratio = cache_misses / total_requests and gate capture. Proposed change to CI sampler: if kv_miss_ratio > ${THRESHOLD} -> full nsys, else perfetto small trace.
Liam O'Neill: Pseudocode for tools/trace_sampler.py:
```python
metrics = read_csv('metrics.csv')
miss_ratio = metrics['cache_misses'] / metrics['total_requests']
if miss_ratio > threshold:
    run('nsys profile --output full.ns --trace=cuda,nvtx --compress')
else:
    run('python -m perfetto_cli --out lightweight.pb --timeline')
```
Samir Patel: Additions: (a) autosplit large nsys artifacts with --max-size flag or do on-the-fly compression, (b) multipart S3 upload + retries in uploader to avoid timeouts.
Julie Ramos: Retention proposal: full raw nsys -> 30 days, lightweight perfetto + normalized JSON -> 90 days, diff artifacts -> 365 days. Update pruning job accordingly.
Ava Chen: Can someone own dashboard ingest changes? need to parse both nsys and perfetto, and apply kernel timeline normalization before diffing. Dashboard prototype: http://dashboards.internal/perf/kvtrace (private).
Liam O'Neill: I'll take dashboard parsing + kernel normalization. Will add a normalization step that maps kernels to op NVTX ids and produces a stable JSON for diffs.
perf-ci-bot: perf-ci-bot: Created tasks: PERF-4321 trace_sampler threshold & compression, PERF-4322 dashboard parser + normalization, PERF-4323 retention & pruning job update.
Julie Ramos: One more: add an NVTX annotation hook in our test harness so traces contain consistent op labels (module.function:op) — helps the grouping.
Ava Chen: Great plan. I'll open the PR for trace_sampler with a canary rollout to nightly for 3 runs, then flip on the sampler globally if stable. Thanks all. :thumbsup:

title: #eng-ml: 1719998888
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — we're planning a 5% staggered rollout of model v5.1-int4-nf4 (bridge variant) tomorrow 10:00 PT. Want to lock the stability gates and auto-fallback thresholds before we flip.
Jordan Patel: Slices we care about? priority customers + longform doc summarization, right?
Ava Chen: Yep. priority customers (acct tier: enterprise), longform (>1024 tokens), and short-chat. Also a generic random sample slice for baseline.
Mike Torres: Quick summary from today's staging shadow run: overall MRR +0.6%, p95 latency +12ms, error rate flat, hallucination rate +0.9 percentage points on longform slice. Small sample though (~6k requests).
Sophia Li: Thanks Mike. That hallucination bump on longform is the main concern. We should set an auto-fallback if hallucination increases by >1.0pp or if MRR drops by >1.5%. Also consider p95 threshold at +30ms absolute.
Jordan Patel: Agree. Need a clear sampling and decision window: propose evaluate every 10k live requests per slice OR every 15 minutes (whichever comes first) during the 5% ramp.
Mike Torres: Also add a quiet-burn guard: if metrics oscillate (sign change) 3 times in a row within the window, pause automated promotion and notify #eng-oncall.
Ava Chen: Sounds good. Can we codify into the rollout policy? Minimal config:
```yaml
rollout:
  phases:
    - pct: 5
      duration: 2h
  evaluation:
    slices: [priority-customers, longform, short-chat, baseline]
    sample_window: 10000
    max_time: 15m
    metrics:
      mrr:
        threshold_down: -1.5 # percent
      hallucination_rate:
        threshold_up: 1.0 # percentage points
      p95_latency_ms:
        threshold_up: 30 # ms
    oscillation_guard: 3 # toggles
    on_failure: auto-fallback
```
Jordan Patel: Minor tweak: make the hallucination threshold 0.9pp for longform slice specifically (more sensitive). For other slices keep 1.0pp.
Sophia Li: Also require that auto-fallback only triggers if the change is statistically significant at p<0.05 or after at least 5k examples in that slice — prevents flip on tiny noise.
Mike Torres: Good call. We'll use bootstrap CI on the difference and require n>=5k per slice. eval-bot will post the CI every window.
Ava Chen: Can someone post the final rollout snippet to the config repo once we agree? I'll schedule the 10:00 PT start and tag infra. @deploy-bot can you do the scheduled deploy and post a follow-up? :thumbsup:
deploy-bot: Scheduled: v5.1-int4-nf4 bridge -> 5% at 2026-03-21T10:00:00-07:00. Will run evaluations per policy and post updates to this thread.
eval-bot: First window (baseline) on shadow run: n=6200, MRR delta +0.6% (95% CI [+0.1, +1.1]), hallucination delta +0.9pp (95% CI [+0.4, +1.4]). Longform slice n=1.8k in shadow -> not enough for auto decision (need 5k).
Jordan Patel: OK — longform needs more burn-in. Keep short-chat and baseline under watch. If longform hits 5k and shows >0.9pp hallucination increase with p<0.05 we rollback.
Sophia Li: I'll add an alert to the console dashboard: threshold crossing + CI check -> auto-fallback action. Also add human-in-loop notify for priority customers.
Ava Chen: Nice. One last item — logging: include sample prompts + model outputs for any hallucination-exceeding examples (scrub PII) and attach to the incident ticket automatically.
Mike Torres: I'll add the logging hook in the inference runtime.
Ava Chen: Great — let's follow up after the first 2-hour phase. Thanks all. :rocket:

title: #eng-platform: 1719876543
preview:
Ravi Patel: FYI — I'm seeing a weird pattern where the routing sampler flips a coin for some requests and our decision logs are empty for those. Tenant: acme-prod.
Ava Chen: which sampler? the global one or the route-level override? :thinking_face:
Ravi Patel: looks like route-level. sampler config shows something like `type: coinflip; p: 0.01` but the traces don't show any eval spans.
Tom G: could be header precedence. We recently added `x-sampling-override: force` and that short-circuits decision logging unless the decision-log flag is explicit.
Ravi Patel: interesting — I captured a request where header `x-sampling`=\"1\" but decision log was absent. curl I used: `curl -H \"Authorization: Bearer XYZ\" -H \"x-sampling: 1\" https://api.redwood.test/v1/route`
Ava Chen: can you paste the route rule? maybe the rule matcher causes a fallback early and the sampler runs after the short-circuit.
Ravi Patel: route: `match: path:/v1/route; header x-tenant: acme.*; actions: forward->modelA; sampler: coinflip(0.01)`
Tom G: hmm. If the gateway applies header overrides before policy evaluation, the sampler decision could be made in a different execution phase and not attached to the decision-log span.
kai-bot: deploy-bot posted: New policy: sampler-eval-phase-change rolled out to 10% of hosts. :information_source:
Ava Chen: that bot means this might be rolled out. good catch. If the eval phase changed, our sampling instrumentation probably needs to copy the decision into the final span.
Ravi Patel: I tried enabling verbose decision logging via `X-Debug-Decision: 1` and then I get logs — but only for ~5% of requests. odd because header value was 1.
Tom G: could be sampling cascade: coinflip -> header override -> final sample. If cascade uses AND/OR incorrectly, header=1 might be ignored unless route-level whitelist is true.
Ava Chen: can you export a sample trace id? we can grep the runtime logs.
Ravi Patel: trace: `tr-20240430-09afa7`. runtime log snippet: `eval_rule route=acme-prod sampler=coinflip p=0.01 decision=1 span_id=...` then nothing about decision-log write.
Tom G: paste the gateway config for decision-logger. maybe the writer has a sampling rate too.
Ravi Patel: `decision_logger: enabled: true; sample_rate: 0.05; inbound_header_override: false` — but we expected inbound_header_override true.
Ava Chen: aha. ops change last week set inbound_header_override:false on canary hosts. that would ignore `x-sampling` header.
Ravi Patel: but I saw header in request so something's inconsistent between envs.
Tom G: possible split: routing and logger run on different host pools. routing on canary (inbound_header_override=false) but logger on stable (honors header). if routing sample happens on canary and logs on stable, decision key may not be carried.
Ravi Patel: makes sense. How do we make decision durable across boundary? we could emit a `decision` header with decision=1, then logger reads it.
Ava Chen: that's the quick fix. long term we should ensure the decision context is included in the tracing envelope. I'll open a tiny ticket.
Ravi Patel: quick repro steps for SRE: 1) hit the route with `x-sampling:1`, 2) capture trace id, 3) check runtime host annotation `canary=true/false`, 4) grep decision_logger events for that trace.
Tom G: I'll add a small inspector in the gateway that writes `_rw_decision` header when sampler runs. patch ready, will roll to canaries.
ops-bot: alert: canary deployment started for gateway-inspector v0.3. :rocket:
Ava Chen: thanks — once that lands we should be able to correlate decisions -> logger writes. Ravi, can you re-run your test after the canary?
Ravi Patel: on it. will post results. :eyes:
Tom G: also let's add a one-line note in the console docs about header precedence and sampler-phase. saved many minutes for next person.
Ava Chen: agreed. small docs blurb + UI flag showing `sampler evaluation phase` on route page.
Ravi Patel: result: after v0.3 canary I see `decision` header and decision-logger entries for previously missing traces. sending follow-up PR to add the console blurb.
Ava Chen: great! :thumbsup:

Maya Patel: Also add a short prompt-eval checklist we can use to baseline quality against redwood-3b-v2. We'll capture before/after samples in the playbook.

Maya Patel: Thanks team. I'll update this thread after I hear back from Dr. Ruiz.

title: #eng-ml: 1718801234
preview:
Marta Liu: Quick sanity check — onboarding llamaX-7b-int8-64k shows a context-window handoff issue when resuming from a saved cache around token ~48000. Cold run matches eval, resumed run diverges after ~3 tokens.
Diego Ramos: Can you repro deterministically? sounds like an index drift in the resume path.
Marta Liu: Yep, deterministic seed 42. Repro steps below, very small script. Cold vs resumed outputs differ starting right after resume.
Marta Liu: ```
# repro (run on staging with model build 2026.03.10-rc3)
python tools/gen.py --model llamaX-7b-int8 --max-tokens 64000 --seed 42 --snapshot out/snap.bin --save-snapshot
python tools/gen.py --model llamaX-7b-int8 --resume out/snap.bin --seed 42 --max-tokens 64005 --out out/resumed.json
python tools/gen.py --model llamaX-7b-int8 --max-tokens 64005 --seed 42 --out out/cold.json
python tools/compare.py out/cold.json out/resumed.json --metric kl --dump-first 10
```
Ava Chen: Thanks — which shard/mapping is this hitting? we did the mmap refactor last week, could be a copy/alignment issue.
Diego Ramos: I ran your script locally on one shard: KL after resume ~0.018 and top1 token mismatch at step +3 consistently. Looks like a one-row offset in the cached key indices after a multi-page mmap+cudaMemcpy.
Sora Patel: Which build? if it's the refactor branch I touched, I can check flags.
Marta Liu: Build tag 2026.03.10-rc3. Also tried quant modes: int8 and q4_0. Curiously int8 reproduced with slightly smaller KL than q4_0.
Diego Ramos: Huh — quant rounding reducing the observed delta is plausible. Also check attention mask alignment on resume: an off-by-one in mask application would produce small but consistent divergence.
Ava Chen: I can schedule a quick bisect across the mmap refactor commit range. Will run on staging cluster and report.
Marta Liu: Eval numbers: LongStoriesBench perplexity cold=9.8, resumed=12.7 (same seed). So it's visible in downstream metrics, not just raw logits.
Diego Ramos: Not huge but consistent. We should add a defensive checksum per-snapshot-chunk to catch index shifts on load.
Sora Patel: I'll audit mmap flags + page alignment + cudaMemcpy offsets tonight. If a page is misaligned we can shift the first KV row by bytes.
ci-bot: Build 2026.03.10-rc3 scheduled: bisect job queued (staging, 5 seeds, 3 quant modes).
Marta Liu: For quick debugging, here are the first 6 logits (softmax) for cold vs resumed at divergence point: cold [0.12,0.08,0.07,0.05,0.03,0.02] resumed [0.11,0.09,0.06,0.06,0.03,0.02] — tiny but repeatable.
Diego Ramos: :thinking_face: ok small drift but deterministic. I'll try adding an assert that compares per-row checksums during resume and log offsets.
Ava Chen: If the assert flags, we can fail fast and capture the raw snapshot bytes for forensic. I'll prepare a quick patch to run on the bisect.
Sora Patel: Plan: 1) reproduce on 3 seeds 2) run bisect across mmap refactor commits 3) add per-chunk CRC guard and re-run 4) run smoke across dedicated cluster if fixed.
Marta Liu: Sounds good. I'll run the initial multi-seed batch now and drop the logs here. Thanks all.
Diego Ramos: Thx — ping if you want me to take the bisect runs.
Ava Chen: I'll coordinate infra runs and collect artifacts into /staging/artifacts/context-handoff/1718801234.
Marta Liu: will update with artifacts and a short summary after runs finish.

preview:

title: #customer-success: 1717509999
preview:
Priya Sharma: Quick ask — can we draft a tiny pulsecard micro-play for AEs to send to customers during week 1? Focus: prefill + small nudge that increases warm-pool hits. Need 2 templates (email & in-app), tracking tag, and a simple signal to mark "prefill success".
Diego Morales: +1. Do we want one for streaming-heavy customers and one for batch-only? Different cache guidance.
Ava Chen: yes — streaming: "start with 3 concurrent streams, enable prefix cache"; batch: "prefill 100 short prompts, TTL 24h". Keep wording non-technical for AEs.
Liam (CS Eng): Drafting now. Proposed in-app template: 'Hey [Name], we primed your workspace with a small set of examples to speed things up. Try <link>. If you see slow responses, reply here and we'll bump your warm pool.'
Priya Sharma: Perfect. Also include a small tracking tag: prefiller=cs_week1_nudge_v1, channel=email|inapp.
Ava Chen: Add fallback cue: if warm-pool hits < 20% after 48h, trigger onboarding task.
Diego Morales: I'll drop a script snippet for engineers to add to onboarding webhook (simple JSON payload).
Diego Morales: ```json
{
  "customer_id":"{{customer_id}}",
  "action":"prefill_request",
  "prefill_tag":"cs_week1_nudge_v1",
  "samples":50
}
```
Liam (CS Eng): Nice. For reporting: add a dashboard row showing prefill_tag + warm_pool_hit_rate over first 7 days.
Priya Sharma: Can someone add this to the shared KB under "microplays > week1 > prefill nudge"? I can sync with AEs tomorrow.
Ava Chen: I'll add the email+in-app copy + the threshold rules. @Diego can you add the webhook snippet to the ops repo and open PR?
Diego Morales: on it. PR link when ready.

Priya Sharma: thanks team :raised_hands: let's keep this short and easy to copy-paste for AEs.

title: #eng-security: 1712345678
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — we're planning to roll out audit schema v3 to staging today. Major goals: clearer principal fields, deterministic resource identifiers for downstream SIEMs, and an opt-in customer validation API.
Ravi Patel: +1. Can you list the breaking changes so Support/docs can prep comms?
Ava Chen: quick list: `actor_id` -> `principal_id` (kept as string), `resource` object shape flattened to `target.type` and `target.id`, `timestamp` now always RFC3339 with timezone, removed `legacy_action` (deprecated). Also added `event_fingerprint` for dedupe.
Samir Khan: Are we changing any RBAC event names? e.g., `role.grant` -> `rbac.grant`? Need to update our detection rules.
Ava Chen: good catch — yes: top-level `action` values were namespaced. Old `role.grant` stays in deprecated list for 30 days, but new canonical is `rbac.grant`. We'll include mapping in changelog.
Elena Gomez: KMS/keys note: encrypted payloads will include header `X-Audit-Signature`. Rotation behaviour: old keys still accepted for signed verification for 7 days after rotation, but events signed with retired keys will be rejected after 30d. Need to mention customer KMS integrations (BYOK).
JP (Support): For customer notices: timeline? How long is the compatibility window and when do we flip read-only enforcement?
Ava Chen: Proposed rollout timeline:
- Day 0 (staging): schema v3 enables in staging, validation endpoint open to customers.
- +7 days (canary): 10% traffic in hosted runs v3 (read+validate).
- +21 days (beta): 100% hosted read in v3, legacy writes accepted but flagged.
- +45 days (enforce): legacy write attempts rejected with 422 and `X-Deprecation` header.
Ravi Patel: Docs action items: add compatibility matrix, diff table, example payloads, and a sample `curl` to hit the validation API. @docs-bot can you create a quick stub?
docs-bot: Created draft page `/docs/audit/schema-v3` with placeholder sections: "Changes", "Migration", "Validation API".
Samir Khan: For internal detection: please include example broken/good payloads. Also please add the mapping for RBAC events and a short note about downstream SIEM field names.
Ava Chen: Putting an example valid v3 event (minimal):
```
{
  "event_id": "evt_01G6T8...",
  "timestamp": "2024-04-03T17:12:03Z",
  "principal_id": "usr_12345",
  "action": "rbac.grant",
  "target": { "type": "project", "id": "proj_987" },
  "event_fingerprint": "sha256:abcd...",
  "metadata": { "ip": "3.86.45.1" }
}
```
Ava Chen: And an example legacy payload that will be deprecated:
```
{
  "event_id": "evt_old_1",
  "timestamp": 1712345603,
  "actor_id": "user-123",
  "action": "role.grant",
  "resource": { "namespace": "projects", "name": "987" }
}
```
JP (Support): For customer-facing validation: what's the public endpoint? Any auth?
Ava Chen: `/v1/audit/validate` in the customer console API. Auth is the same API key + optional HMAC signature for BYOK customers. Response codes: 200 (valid), 422 (schema errors) with `errors` array, 409 (fingerprint collision). We'll include examples in docs.
Ravi Patel: Communication draft: short email + changelog entry + top-of-console banner for customers with heavy audit usage. Also update the webhook contract doc for customers who pull events.
Elena Gomez: Security checklist for rollout: verify KMS signing acceptance, confirm RBAC event coverage matrix (admin/role/permission changes), run vuln scan on the validation service, and make sure audit ingestion queue has back-pressure handling with dead-lettering.
Samir Khan: On the vuln triage side: I ran a quick scan of the validator and found one low CVE in json-schema lib pinned at old patch. I'll open PR to bump it.
Ava Chen: thanks — add that PR link to the release checklist. Also we should add a short customer FAQ covering: "Will this break my SIEM?" "Can I opt-out?" "How to map old fields?"
JP (Support): I'll draft the FAQ and add canned responses for SEs. Also planning a gamma customer outreach to 3 top audit-heavy customers to try the validation endpoint.
Ravi Patel: Last: telemetry/metrics we want to track during canary: `audit.validate.errors`, `audit.legacy.write_attempts`, `audit.signature.failures`. Add them to console SLO dashboard.
deploy-bot: Deployed `audit-schema-v3-validator` to staging (build 2024.23.7).
Ava Chen: Perfect. Next steps: Ravi to own docs & comms, Samir to patch deps and prepare mapping table, JP to coordinate customer trials, Elena to sign off security checklist. I'll open the changelog PR and include the customer-facing migration guide.
Ravi Patel: I'll add console banner copy and the short email draft to the PR.
Samir Khan: I'll link PR -> vulnerability ticket and add unit tests for the mapping helpers.
JP (Support): I'll queue outreach emails and set a calendar invite with the 3 customers.
Elena Gomez: I'll run the final KMS acceptance tests tomorrow morning and report back here.
Ava Chen: Thanks everyone — posting meeting notes to the audit-schema-v3 project board after this thread. :thumbsup:

title: #product: 1711111111
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — proposing an experiment: shift to a UX-led handoff for design partners to reduce dropouts and tighten feedback loops. UX sends a contextual invite + micro-survey, then hands off to PM/Eng for SLA/creds. Goal: fewer no-shows, faster first feedback. Thoughts?
Liam O'Connor: Love it. Can we A/B the new flow against current invite so we have a control?
Rach Patel: Agree with A/B. Also we should tweak the reward matrix — higher-effort partners should get more credit. And capture expected session length in the invite so expectations are set.
Samir Gupta: Heads up — sandbox creds provisioning is manual today (ticket). If we rely on handoff automation we'll need an auto-provision path. I can do a small service to provision creds but need 10 business days if prioritized.
Maria (PM): +1 to A/B. Let's pick clear success metrics: 30% reduction in no-shows, median time-to-first-feedback <48h, and qualitative satisfaction (NPS-ish) target >=8. Keep it focused.
Ava Chen: Draft micro-survey (super short):
```
1) What best describes your team? [Product/Platform/Startup/Other]
2) Typical session length? [15m/30m/60m/Other]
3) Are you able to run a short eval in a sandbox? [Yes/No]
```
Rach Patel: Good. For reward tiers I propose: 15m -> $25 credit, 30m -> $50, 60m -> $150 + priority support. We can bold the higher reward for 60m to nudge long sessions.
Samir Gupta: Auto-provisioning plan (MVP): POST /provision_dp_sandbox -> infra job -> returns temporary creds. Security: generate customer-scoped role, KMS-tagged keys, rotate TTL 24h. ETA ~10 business days if we add as P1.
Ava Chen: Decision proposal: run A/B for 2 weeks; prioritize automation MVP (sandbox endpoint) as P1 for DP cohort. Action items: @Samir build endpoint, @Rach update invite copy + reward table, @Maria define A/B cohorts & metrics, I'll draft UX flows and invite templates.
Maria (PM): I'll spin up the experiment doc and kickoff calendar. Can someone add me to the tracking sheet once it's up?
Liam O'Connor: I'll create a small tracking sheet and add Maria; will also wire a column for cohort assignment + outcome metrics. :thumbsup:
ProdBot: Reminder: DP automation task added to sprint backlog (ticket #DP-3789).
Ava Chen: Thanks all — sync check on Friday 11:00 to review progress and confirm A/B buckets. :rocket:
